question: What deployment models do you support: SaaS, on-premise, hybrid? Where does our data go in each model?
title: Deployment Models and Data Residency Transparency
bullets: List all supported deployment models (SaaS, on-premise, hybrid) with clear definitions
Explain end-to-end data flow for each model, including storage, processing, and transit
Specify data residency options (regions, customer control, regulatory alignment)
Clarify differences in control, responsibility, and risk across deployment models
suggested_inputs: Architecture diagrams for SaaS, on-premise, and hybrid deployments
Data flow diagrams with classification (PII, logs, metadata)
Customer deployment references or case studies

good.text: We support SaaS, on-premise, and hybrid deployments. In SaaS, data is processed in customer-selected regions with strict residency controls. In on-premise, all sensitive data remains within the customer environment, with optional metadata sent for analytics. Hybrid allows policy enforcement on-prem while aggregated telemetry is processed in the cloud. We provide detailed diagrams for each model.

We offer SaaS deployment with region-specific processing and storage. Customers can select regions (e.g., EU, US, APAC), and data never leaves the chosen boundary. We provide data residency guarantees aligned with GDPR and similar regulations.

We guide customers based on regulatory needs: for GDPR, we recommend EU-hosted SaaS or on-premise; for highly regulated environments, on-premise ensures full control. Each model includes documented data flows and control boundaries.

In our hybrid model, sensitive payload data remains on-premise, while anonymized metadata and policy logs are sent to the cloud for analytics. Customers can configure what data is shared and retain full control over sensitive information.

partial.text: We support SaaS and on-premise deployments, but the exact data flow depends on the configuration and customer setup.
We are primarily SaaS-based and store data in our cloud environment, with some regional options available.
We offer hybrid deployments, though the split between on-premise and cloud processing may vary and is not strictly defined.
Customer data is handled securely across all deployment models, but specific data flow details are not always documented.

bad.text: We are SaaS-only and do not provide details on data residency or regional control.
All customer data is stored and processed in the US regardless of customer location.
We cannot clearly describe how data flows across different deployment models.
We support on-premise deployments, but we do not have active customers using this model.

follow_ups: For SaaS: Which cloud providers and regions are supported? Can customers explicitly select and restrict regions?
For on-premise: What is the deployment complexity and how many customers are actively using this model?
For hybrid: What specific data remains on-premise versus sent to the cloud? Can customers configure this boundary?
Provide architecture diagrams for each deployment model with data classification (PII, metadata, logs) clearly mapped.
